description: <One paragraph: what the CLI wraps, what it produces, and who calls it. This is the blurb context7 shows in search results, so lead with the concrete capability rather than the company.>
rules: TEMPLATE — replace these with 5 to 10 real pitfalls. Rules earn their place by describing something an agent gets wrong when reasoning from the API alone; anything obvious from reading the command catalog does not belong here.
State the machine-readable output switch and that the default output is for humans.
State how credentials are supplied and what the failure looks like when they are missing.
State the exit-code contract, including any failure that does NOT change the exit code.
State which identifier addresses an object and where that value comes from in earlier output.
State anything the CLI refuses to do, so an agent stops instead of retrying.
